alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study employed a deep neural network, DnCNN, to effectively reduce noise in immunohistochemistry images.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Denoising Convolutional Neural Network (DnCNN) effectively reduces noise in immunohistochemistry images.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the preprint's technical contribution identically, with no change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: On day 3 and day 7, negligible nerve fibers were present throughout the wound, with few only on the lateral boundaries.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study established a quantitative time course of re-innervation in wound healing.
alignments.rationale: The publication summarizes the detailed temporal observations as a quantitative time course; the specific day-by-day details are subsumed into that broader result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A slight increase in nerve fiber density was observed on day 10, which significantly increased on day 15.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study established a quantitative time course of re-innervation in wound healing.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same temporal progression but frames it as part of an overall quantitative time course rather than listing specific day values in the claim list.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a positive correlation (R² = 0.933) between nerve fiber density and re-epithelization, suggesting an association between re-innervation and re-epithelization.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A positive correlation (R2�=�0.926) exists between nerve fiber density and re-epithelization during wound healing.
alignments.rationale: Both versions report the same positive correlation between nerve fiber density and re-epithelization; the numerical R2 differs slightly but does not change the conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The automated image analysis method provides a novel and useful tool for quantifying innervation in the skin and other tissues.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The automated image analysis method provides a novel and useful tool for quantifying innervation in skin and other tissues.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the contribution claim but lists its certainty as medium rather than high, indicating a slight tempering of confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The CLR transformation is a sample-wise operation that cannot, in itself, 'leak' information or invalidate downstream analyses.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The CLR transformation can create phenotype-associated features from initially sparse data without indicating information leakage.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the preprint's assertion to emphasize that CLR can produce phenotype-associated features without that implying leakage, which clarifies but does not contradict the original conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: When the geometric mean is associated with a phenotype, sparse and CLR-transformed features will also become associated with it.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The phenomenon of sparse features becoming phenotype-associated can also be observed after a CLR transformation.
alignments.rationale: This publication claim restates the preprint result directly: CLR-transformed (and sparse) features inherit associations when the geometric mean is associated with phenotype.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The phenomenon of sparse features becoming phenotype-associated can also be observed after a CLR transformation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The phenomenon of sparse features becoming phenotype-associated can also be observed after a CLR transformation.
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces this specific observation verbatim, so there is no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The detection of phenotype-associated features that were initially sparse is not independently sufficient to demonstrate information leakage in machine learning pipelines.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The detection of phenotype-associated features that were initially sparse is not independently sufficient to demonstrate information leakage in machine learning pipelines.
alignments.rationale: The publication concurs exactly with the preprint's conclusion about the insufficiency of such detections to prove information leakage.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Mirror-symmetric viewpoint tuning for faces emerges in the fully connected layers of convolutional deep neural networks trained on object recognition tasks, even when the training dataset does not include faces.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Mirror-symmetric viewpoint tuning for faces emerges in the fully connected layers of convolutional deep neural networks trained on object recognition tasks, even when the training dataset does not include faces.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this result verbatim; there is no change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Mirror-symmetric viewpoint tuning in convolutional neural network models is not unique to faces: it emerges for multiple object categories with bilateral symmetry.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Mirror-symmetric viewpoint tuning in convolutional network models is not unique to faces: it emerges for multiple object categories with bilateral symmetry.
alignments.rationale: Both documents state the same result that mirror-symmetric tuning appears for multiple bilaterally symmetric categories.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Learning to discriminate among bilaterally symmetric object categories induces reflection-equivariant intermediate representations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Learning to discriminate among bilaterally symmetric object categories induces reflection-equivariant intermediate representations.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this conclusion with equivalent wording and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AL-like mirror-symmetric tuning is achieved when reflection-equivariant responses are spatially pooled by downstream units with sufficiently large receptive fields.
alignments.pub_claim_text: AL-like mirror-symmetric tuning is achieved when equivariant responses are spatially pooled by downstream units with sufficiently large receptive fields.
alignments.rationale: The publication states the same mechanistic result but with higher reported certainty (medium->high), so confidence is amplified while the semantic content is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our theory predicts that mirror-symmetric viewpoint tuning can emerge as a consequence of exposure to bilaterally symmetric objects beyond the category of faces.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our theory predicts that mirror-symmetric viewpoint tuning can emerge as a consequence of exposure to bilaterally symmetric objects beyond the category of faces, and that it can generalize beyond previously experienced object categories.
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the theoretical prediction to additionally claim generalization beyond previously experienced categories and reports higher confidence, broadening and amplifying the original low-confidence prediction.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepTaskGen generates non-acquired task-based contrast maps from resting-state fMRI data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepTaskGen achieves superior reconstruction performance while retaining inter-individual variation essential for biomarker development.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the generation capability in terms of superior reconstruction performance and retention of inter-individual variation, which directly corresponds to the preprint's generation claim without changing its scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepTaskGen outperforms several benchmarks in generating synthetic task-contrast maps.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepTaskGen outperforms several benchmarks in generating synthetic task-contrast maps.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this result claim directly and with the same scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Synthetic task contrast maps achieved similar or greater performance compared to actual task contrast maps and resting-state connectomes for predicting a wide range of demographic, cognitive, and clinical variables.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Synthetic maps generated by DeepTaskGen demonstrate comparable or superior predictive performance relative to actual maps and rs-connectomes across diverse demographic, cognitive, and clinical variables.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the core comparative predictive-performance claim but reports this as a medium-certainty result rather than the preprint's higher certainty, indicating a slight tempering of confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach will facilitate the study of individual differences and the generation of task-related biomarkers.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepTaskGen facilitates the study of individual differences and the generation of task-related biomarkers.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the conclusion and assigns it higher certainty (medium->high), so the semantic content is unchanged but confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepPBS is a geometric deep-learning model designed to predict binding specificity across protein families based on protein-DNA structures. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepPBS is a geometric deep-learning model designed to predict binding specificity from protein–DNA structure. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication claim is essentially the same contribution statement as the preprint, with no substantive change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepPBS can calculate protein heavy atom-level importance scores, which conform well with alanine scanning mutagenesis experimental data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Interpretable protein heavy atom importance scores for interface residues can be extracted from DeepPBS. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the extraction of atom-level importance scores but omits the explicit claim that these scores conform well to alanine scanning data, reducing the strength/scope of the validation claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The inference time for DeepPBS is sufficiently fast for analyzing simulation trajectories. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report or claim anything about inference speed or suitability for analyzing simulation trajectories, so this performance claim was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepPBS offers a foundation for machine-aided protein-DNA interaction studies, guiding experimental choices and complex design. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepPBS offers a foundation for machine-aided studies that advance our understanding of molecular interactions and guide experimental designs and synthetic biology. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the foundational/concluding claim but broadens the stated impact (explicitly naming synthetic biology and understanding molecular interactions), clarifying and slightly broadening the application.

alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepPBS can be applied to experimental or predicted structures. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This application-to-predicted-structures claim appears in the publication but was not present in the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepPBS was demonstrated to predict experimentally measured binding specificity when applied to designed proteins targeting specific DNA sequences. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a demonstration on designed proteins targeting specific sequences that was not stated in the preprint, so this is an added experimental result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Training a neural network on FoldX output significantly improves prediction accuracy for protein folding and binding free energies. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The dFX model improves the prediction accuracy of free energy changes (ΔΔG) relative to FoldX. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates that the learned correction improves ΔΔG predictions relative to FoldX, matching the preprint's main result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The correlation for single mutations affecting folding improved from 0.3 to 0.66 after applying the neural network model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The dFX correction leads to improvements in Pearson correlation between experimental and predicted ΔΔG across folding and binding tasks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the same directional claim about improved Pearson correlation but omits the exact numerical improvement reported in the preprint, weakening the specificity of the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Models trained on double mutations enhance accuracy when predicting higher-order mutations, while models trained on single mutations do not. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Models trained on lower-order mutations retain predictive capability for higher-order mutations in binding. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses a more general statement that lower-order-trained models can retain predictive capability for higher-order binding mutations, which alters the preprint's specific claim about double-vs-single training performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed models add minimal computational time but provide a substantial increase in performance, particularly for higher-order mutations and epistasis. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The dFX model trained on single mutations for binding outperformed FoldX and other machine-learning approaches when tested on an external SARS-CoV-2 data set. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication highlights a specific external-dataset outperformance not in the preprint and does not reiterate claims about minimal computational overhead or broad improvements for higher-order mutations/epistasis, shifting the emphasis of the result.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The dFX approach could be further optimized to predict antibody escape, aiding in the efficient development of watch lists. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This forward-looking application to antibody escape and watch-list development appears in the publication but was not present in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed self-supervised pretraining technique improved the segmentation performance from 0.67 to 0.70 of Object-wise Intersection over Union. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed self-supervised pretraining technique improved the segmentation performance from 0.67 to 0.70 of object-wise intersection over union. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numeric improvement and metric are identical between preprint and publication, indicating the result was preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Specialised post-processing pipelines with tunable/optimizable parameters were designed for each segmentation strategy. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A novel U-Net-based method for robust adherent cell segmentation for quantitative phase microscopy images was designed and optimized. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication describes an optimized segmentation method but does not explicitly detail the per-strategy tunable post-processing pipelines; the contribution appears retained but expressed at a higher level.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Transferability to different cell types was increased by the optimisation of post-processing parameters. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly claim improved transferability across cell types due to post-processing optimization, so this specific transferability claim was not carried forward.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A new manually labelled quantitative phase imaging dataset for cell segmentation was created, along with unlabelled data for self-supervised pretraining. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A new dataset of manually labelled images suitable for this task was published along with unlabelled data for self-supervised pretraining. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The dataset publication claim is retained with the same content about labeled and unlabeled data for pretraining.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In all layers of the CNN models, there were artificial neurons that responded consistently and selectively to neutral, pleasant, or unpleasant images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Artificial neurons in CNN models responded consistently and selectively to neutral, pleasant, or unpleasant images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the finding that CNN units showed consistent, selective affective responses, matching the preprint result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Lesioning these neurons by setting their output to 0 led to decreased emotion recognition performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Lesioning or enhancing artificial neurons in CNN models affected emotion recognition performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication combines lesioning and enhancement effects into a single claim but preserves the lesioning result that performance was affected.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Enhancing these neurons by increasing their gain led to increased emotion recognition performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Lesioning or enhancing artificial neurons in CNN models affected emotion recognition performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the enhancement result within its combined statement that manipulating units modulates emotion recognition performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These results support the idea that the visual system may have the intrinsic ability to represent the affective significance of visual input. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The visual cortex has an intrinsic ability to represent the affective significance of visual input. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the conclusion more strongly and with higher certainty (asserting the visual cortex has this ability) compared with the preprint's more tentative wording that results support the idea.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CNNs offer a fruitful platform for testing neuroscientific theories. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: CNNs provide a fruitful platform for testing neuroscientific theories. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the contribution claim that CNNs are a useful platform for testing neuroscientific theories.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Self-Supervised Mutual Infomax Graph Convolution Network (MiGCN) predicts disease-gene associations effectively by utilizing external disease-disease and gene-gene collaborative graphs. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Self-Supervised Mutual Infomax Graph Convolution Network (MiGCN) predicts disease-gene associations effectively by utilizing external disease-disease and gene-gene collaborative graphs. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the main contribution identically to the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method developed in this study eliminates noise within collaborative graphs by maximizing the mutual information between nodes and neighbors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method developed in this study eliminates noise within collaborative graphs by maximizing the mutual information between nodes and neighbors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the methodological result about noise reduction via mutual information maximization.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MiGCN demonstrates a performance improvement of more than 8% on AUC compared to the state-of-the-art method. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: MiGCN demonstrates a performance improvement of more than 8% on AUC compared to the state-of-the-art method. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The quantitative performance improvement claim is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The learning ability of the graph neural network is enhanced through a novel informative message passing layer. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication introduces a specific architectural rationale (an informative message passing layer) that was not stated in the preprint, so this is an added claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning models can predict how the concentration of dosage-sensitive transcription factors affects regulatory element chromatin accessibility in facial progenitor cells with accuracy approaching experimental reproducibility.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Transfer learning can predict how concentrations of the dosage-sensitive TFs TWIST1 and SOX9 affect regulatory element chromatin accessibility in facial progenitor cells with near-experimental accuracy.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the predictive result but specifies the TFs (TWIST1 and SOX9), which narrows the scope while preserving the core claim about near-experimental accuracy.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: High-affinity motifs that allow for heterotypic transcription factor co-binding buffer against quantitative changes in transcription factor dosage and strongly predict unperturbed accessibility.
alignments.pub_claim_text: High-affinity motifs that allow for heterotypic TF co-binding buffer against quantitative changes in TF dosage and predict unperturbed accessibility.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the buffering/predictive role of high-affinity heterotypic motifs but tones down the emphatic phrasing ('strongly') leading to a slight tempering of confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Motifs with low-affinity or homotypic binding lead to sensitive responses to transcription factor dosage with minimal contributions to unperturbed accessibility.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Low-affinity or homotypic binding motifs drive sensitive responses with minimal impact on unperturbed accessibility.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same relationship between low-affinity/homotypic motifs and sensitive responses with minimal effect on baseline accessibility without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Both buffering and sensitizing features of transcription factor motifs show signatures of purifying selection.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Both buffering and sensitizing features of TF binding motifs display purifying selection signatures.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same evolutionary conclusion about purifying selection on both buffering and sensitizing motif features with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The combination of transfer learning and quantitative measurements of chromatin response to transcription factor dosage reveals additional layers of the cis-regulatory code.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The combination of transfer learning and quantitative chromatin response measurements provides a novel approach for uncovering additional layers of the cis-regulatory code.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the same contribution as a 'novel approach' and otherwise preserves the claim that transfer learning plus quantitative measurements reveal additional cis-regulatory features.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Polaris is an analysis pipeline for image-based spatial transcriptomics that combines deep learning models for cell segmentation and spot detection.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Polaris is an analysis pipeline for image-based spatial transcriptomics that combines deep-learning models for cell segmentation and spot detection.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same description of Polaris as a pipeline combining deep-learning segmentation and spot detection models with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Polaris quantifies single-cell gene expression accurately using a probabilistic gene decoder.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Polaris quantifies single-cell gene expression accurately using a probabilistic gene decoder.
alignments.rationale: The publication maintains the same result about accurate single-cell quantification via a probabilistic gene decoder.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Polaris provides a unifying, turnkey solution for analyzing spatial transcriptomics data from MERFSIH, seqFISH, or ISS experiments.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Polaris provides a unifying, turnkey solution for analyzing spatial transcriptomics data from various experimental methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication generalizes the claim (from specific methods to various methods), broadening scope while preserving the turnkey/unifying contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The 'Hacking the Human Body' machine learning algorithm development competition resulted in models that successfully segment anatomical structures across five organs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Teams overcame challenges posed by tissue variations in the dataset by using color normalization techniques and combining vision transformers with convolutional models.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes techniques teams used to overcome dataset challenges rather than explicitly claiming broad successful segmentation across five organs, reducing specificity and emphasis on success.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: 1,175 teams from 78 countries participated in the competition, demonstrating significant global engagement in open-science code development.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Hacking the Human Body machine learning algorithm development competition engaged 1175 teams from 78 countries.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the participation metrics directly, preserving the contribution claim about broad engagement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The machine learning models developed during the competition will be productized in the HuBMAP data portal to process large datasets for Human Reference Atlas construction.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The best model from the competition will be productized in the HuBMAP portal to process tissue image datasets at scale.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies that the 'best model' (singular) will be productized rather than plural 'models', narrowing scope; the statement is presented with higher certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The competition created benchmark data that highlights major challenges faced by participants in the segmentation task.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A dataset containing 880 histology images with 12,901 segmented structures was created for the competition.
alignments.rationale: The publication quantifies the benchmark dataset created (images and segmented structures), clarifying and strengthening the dataset-creation aspect of the preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SMAdd-seq can profile chromatin accessibility by labeling accessible DNA regions with angelicin.
alignments.pub_claim_text: SMAdd-seq can profile chromatin accessibility by labeling accessible DNA regions with angelicin.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the core methodological claim about SMAdd-seq labeling accessible DNA with angelicin without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: NEMO accurately called expected nucleosome occupancy patterns near transcription start sites at both bulk and single-molecule levels.
alignments.pub_claim_text: NEMO accurately called expected nucleosome occupancy patterns near transcription start sites at both bulk and single-molecule levels.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same result about NEMO's accurate nucleosome occupancy calling at both bulk and single-molecule resolution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SMAdd-seq performs equivalently on purified yeast nuclei and intact cells.
alignments.pub_claim_text: SMAdd-seq performs equivalently on purified yeast nuclei and intact cells, indicating its promise for in vivo chromatin labeling.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the equivalence result and additionally comments on in vivo promise; the core equivalence claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method indicates the potential for in vivo chromatin labeling on long single molecules to measure native chromatin dynamics and heterogeneity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: SMAdd-seq performs equivalently on purified yeast nuclei and intact cells, indicating its promise for in vivo chromatin labeling.
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same prospective conclusion about SMAdd-seq's promise for in vivo chromatin labeling, combining it with the equivalence observation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: lociPARSE is a locality-aware invariant point attention architecture for scoring RNA 3D structures.
alignments.pub_claim_text: lociPARSE is a locality-aware invariant point attention architecture for scoring RNA 3D structures.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution statement for lociPARSE with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: lociPARSE estimates Local Distance Difference Test (lDDT) scores in a superposition-free manner.
alignments.pub_claim_text: lociPARSE estimates Local Distance Difference Test (lDDT) scores in a superposition-free manner.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the methodological claim about superposition-free lDDT estimation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: lociPARSE significantly outperforms existing statistical potentials and machine learning methods across complementary assessment metrics.
alignments.pub_claim_text: lociPARSE significantly outperforms existing statistical potentials and machine learning methods across complementary assessment metrics.
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the performance improvement claim with consistent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: High-fidelity RNA scoring has proven to be difficult using conventional knowledge-based statistical potentials and currently-available machine learning-based approaches.
alignments.pub_claim_text: High-fidelity RNA scoring has proven to be difficult using conventional knowledge-based statistical potentials and currently available machine learning-based approaches.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the limitation/challenge statement about existing RNA scoring approaches with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ProteinMPNN had a similar success rate to Rosetta, yielding 13 new experimentally confirmed assemblies. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ProteinMPNN had a similar success rate to Rosetta, yielding 13 new experimentally confirmed assemblies. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication claim is word-for-word the same result as the preprint, with no change in scope or expressed confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ProteinMPNN required orders of magnitude less computation and no manual refinement compared to Rosetta. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ProteinMPNN required orders of magnitude less computation and no manual refinement compared to Rosetta. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same comparative computational-effort claim with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The interfaces designed by ProteinMPNN were substantially more polar than those designed by Rosetta, facilitating in vitro assembly of the designed nanomaterials. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The interfaces designed by ProteinMPNN were substantially more polar than those designed by Rosetta, facilitating in vitro assembly of the designed nanomaterials. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Text is effectively identical; only the article labels the statement as a conclusion rather than a result, with no substantive change in content or certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Crystal structures of several of the assemblies confirmed the accuracy of the design method at high resolution. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Crystal structures of several of the assemblies confirmed the accuracy of the design method at high resolution. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical claim about crystal structure validation and high-resolution confirmation; no change in meaning or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our results showcase the potential of deep learning-based methods to unlock the widespread application of designed protein-protein interfaces and self-assembling protein nanomaterials in biotechnology. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results showcase the potential of deep learning-based methods to unlock the widespread application of designed protein-protein interfaces and self-assembling protein nanomaterials in biotechnology. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Conclusion wording is effectively the same between preprint and publication, retaining the same scope and expressed certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The JAX Animal Behavior System (JABS) facilitates data acquisition, machine learning-based behavior annotation, and genetic analysis. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The JAX Animal Behavior System (JABS) provides an integrated platform for data acquisition, machine learning-based behavior annotation, and genetic analysis. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Both statements express the same contribution; the publication rephrases as an 'integrated platform' but does not change the underlying claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The JABS Data Acquisition Module (JABS-DA) enables uniform data collection through its combination of 3D hardware designs and software. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The JABS Data Acquisition Module (JABS-DA) enables uniform data collection through its combination of 3D hardware designs and software for real-time monitoring. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds the specific capability of 'real-time monitoring,' clarifying the module's functionality while retaining the original claim and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel graph-based framework (ethograph) allows efficient boutwise comparison of JABS-AL classifiers. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel graph-based framework (ethograph) allows efficient boutwise comparison of JABS-AL classifiers. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The ethograph claim is identical between versions with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The JABS-Analysis and Integration Module (JABS-AI) supports the inference and sharing of trained classifiers and downstream genetic analyses. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The JABS-Analysis and Integration Module (JABS-AI) facilitates the deployment and sharing of trained classifiers, supporting downstream genetic analyses. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication rewords the module's role to emphasize 'deployment' in addition to sharing and inference; this clarifies operational use without changing the claim's scope or certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This open-source tool reduces the barrier to entry into advanced behavior analysis for the neuroscience and genetics community. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This open-source tool reduces the barrier to entry for the neuroscience and genetics community in advanced behavior analysis. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Substantively the same concluding claim; wording minorly rearranged but meaning and certainty are retained.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PathFinder is a novel graph transformer model designed to infer multi-cell intra- and inter-cellular signaling pathways and communications. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: PathFinder is a novel graph transformer model designed to infer multi-cell intra- and inter-cellular signaling pathways and communications among multi-cell types. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Both describe the same contribution; the publication adds the phrase 'among multi-cell types' but does not alter the claim's substance or certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PathFinder utilizes a divide-and-conquer strategy to divide complex signaling networks into signaling paths for scoring and ranking. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: PathFinder employs a divide-and-conquer strategy to divide complex signaling networks into signaling paths for scoring and ranking. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The methodological description is the same between preprint and publication with only a minor wording change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Evaluation of PathFinder using scRNA-seq data from APOE4-genotype specific AD mice models identified novel APOE4 altered intra- and inter-cell interaction networks among neurons, astrocytes, and microglia. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The Shannon equation yielded an accuracy of 63.9% using a k value of 1.79. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same interpretive conclusion with matching content and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A graph convolutional network (GCN) can directly learn from tree sequence topology and node data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A graph convolutional network can directly learn from tree sequence topology and node data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same technical result with equivalent specificity and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The GCN approach performs well on common population genetics inference tasks, with accuracies roughly matching or exceeding that of a CNN-based method. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The graph convolutional network approach performs well on common population genetic inference tasks, with accuracies roughly matching or exceeding those of a convolutional neural network-based method. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same performance comparison and level of confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Tree sequences are not interchangeable with existing data structures used for population genetic inference methodologies. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The specific statement about non-interchangeability does not appear in the publication; no close counterpart is present.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed GCN allows for the use of neural network applications without converting tree sequences to population genetic alignment format. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Tree sequences can be utilized in population genetic inference without the need to convert them to population genetic alignment format. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same capability but presents it with slightly lower stated certainty/weight, so the claim is retained but tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Squidiff is a diffusion model-based generative framework that predicts transcriptomic changes across diverse cell types in response to environmental changes. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Squidiff is a diffusion model-based generative framework that predicts transcriptomic changes across diverse cell types in response to environmental changes. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution claim with equivalent wording and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Squidiff demonstrates robustness across cell differentiation, gene perturbation, and drug response prediction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Squidiff demonstrates robustness across cell differentiation, gene perturbation, and drug response prediction. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is restated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Squidiff enables in silico screening of molecular landscapes, facilitating rapid hypothesis generation. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Squidiff enables in silico screening of molecular landscapes and cellular state transitions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the claim by explicitly adding cellular state transitions and frames it more as a demonstrated result, so the claim is clarified and slightly broadened.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Squidiff provides valuable insights for precision medicine. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Squidiff provides valuable insights into the regulatory principles of cell fate decisions. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication shifts the claimed insight from a broad precision-medicine application to a more specific mechanistic insight into cell-fate regulation, narrowing the scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepRescore2 increases the number of correctly identified peptide-spectrum matches by 17% in a synthetic dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepRescore2 increases the number of correctly identified peptide-spectrum matches by 17% in a synthetic dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces the exact quantitative result and wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepRescore2 identifies 19%-46% more phosphopeptides in biological datasets compared to a state-of-the-art computational workflow. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepRescore2 identifies 19% to 46% more phosphopeptides in biological datasets compared to a state-of-the-art computational workflow. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same quantitative performance range and comparison.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: 30% of the significantly altered phosphosites between tumor and normal tissues identified from DeepRescore2-processed data could not be identified based on the state-of-the-art workflow. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: 30% of the significantly altered phosphosites between tumor and normal tissues identified from DeepRescore2-processed data could not be identified based on the state-of-the-art workflow. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this exact comparative discovery statistic.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DeepRescore2 uniquely identifies EGFR hyperactivation as a new target in poor-prognosis liver cancer, which is validated experimentally. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DeepRescore2 uniquely identifies EGFR hyperactivation as a new target in poor-prognosis liver cancer, which is validated experimentally. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the novel biological finding and experimental validation; wording and confidence remain aligned.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Integration of deep learning prediction in DeepRescore2 improves phosphopeptide identification and facilitates biological discoveries. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The general concluding claim about integration facilitating discoveries is not explicitly restated in the publication separate from the specific results, so no direct counterpart is present.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Cytotoxic T lymphocytes from a convalescent patient targeting an epitope conserved across all known variants of concern are serial killers capable of eliminating multiple infected targets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Cytotoxic T lymphocytes from a convalescent patient are capable of eliminating multiple infected target cells. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the killing-multiple-targets result but omits the explicit phrasing about the conserved-epitope across all variants and the 'serial killer' framing, reducing specificity.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: T cells specific for a conserved epitope within the SARS-CoV-2 spike protein can rapidly eliminate virally infected targets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study provides the first direct evidence of the effector functions of T cells targeting a conserved epitope across all known variants of concern. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same key finding about T cells specific for a conserved epitope and its effector function, emphasizing novelty but not changing substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The ability to map the functional capacity of T cells and their ability to kill infected cells provides fundamental insights into the immunology of vaccines and recovery from infections. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings have implications for vaccine development and for the recovery and monitoring of infected individuals. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication states the same implications for vaccines and recovery/monitoring, matching the preprint conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The imaging platform using artificial intelligence can track thousands of individual cell-cell interactions within nanowell arrays. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include an explicit claim about the imaging platform or its AI-based high-throughput tracking, so this contribution appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Gauge equivariant convolutional neural network (gCNN) layers can overcome the challenges associated with diffusion MRI signal acquisition on a sphere. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Gauge equivariant convolutional neural network (gCNN) layers can overcome the challenges associated with diffusion MRI signal acquisition on a sphere. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same methodological claim with equivalent specificity and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method can upsample angular resolution for predicting diffusion tensor imaging (DTI) parameters from just six diffusion gradient directions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed gCNN method allows for upsampling angular resolution for predicting diffusion tensor imaging (DTI) parameters from just six diffusion gradient directions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same quantitative capability (upsampling from six directions) and result statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: gCNNs allow for training with fewer subjects compared to a baseline model that uses only 3D convolutions. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: gCNNs can train with fewer subjects compared to a baseline model that involves only 3D convolutions. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same comparative training-data efficiency claim with similar certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Cryogenic electron microscopy (CryoEM) produces structures of macromolecules at near-atomic resolution.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not restate the general claim about CryoEM resolution; it omits this background statement present in the preprint, so the claim is removed in the published version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Building molecular models with good stereochemical geometry from CryoEM structures can be challenging and time-consuming.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include this statement about modeling difficulty and instead focuses on the presented protocol, so the preprint conclusion is not carried forward.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The presented model refinement protocol automatically generates series of molecular models from CryoEM datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A model refinement protocol is presented that automatically generates series of molecular models from CryoEM datasets.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this contribution verbatim/near-verbatim, indicating no substantive change in meaning, scope, or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The generated molecular models describe the dynamics of the macromolecular system and have near-perfect geometry scores.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The generated molecular models describe the dynamics of the macromolecular system and have near-perfect geometry scores.
alignments.rationale: This result is carried over exactly in the publication, preserving content and confidence.

alignments.pub_claim_text: This method facilitates the interpretation of protein complex movement from heterogeneity analysis.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a claim about facilitating interpretation of complex movement that was not present in the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The structural dynamics observed from CryoEM data can be compared with results from other experimental and simulation techniques.
alignments.rationale: The publication introduces a new claim about comparing CryoEM-derived dynamics with other experimental and simulation results absent from the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PerSEveML is an interactive web-based tool that uses crowd-sourced intelligence to predict rare events and determine feature selection structures.
alignments.pub_claim_text: PerSEveML is an interactive web-based tool that uses crowd-sourced intelligence to predict rare events and determine feature selection structures.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this description of PerSEveML with the same scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PerSEveML provides evaluation metrics that help users understand the contribution of individual machine learning methods to the prediction process.
alignments.pub_claim_text: PerSEveML provides a comprehensive overview of the integrative approach through evaluation metrics that help users understand the contribution of individual ML methods to the prediction process.
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the wording (adds 'comprehensive overview of the integrative approach') but retains the same core claim about evaluation metrics and their role, clarifying rather than changing scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PerSEveML has been evaluated on three diverse biologically complex data sets with extremely rare events and has demonstrated its ability to generate valid hypotheses.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We have evaluated PerSEveML on three diverse biologically complex data sets with extremely rare events and have demonstrated its ability to generate valid hypotheses.
alignments.rationale: The evaluation and conclusion about hypothesis generation are restated directly in the publication without substantive change.

alignments.pub_claim_text: PerSEveML calculates entropy and rank scores, which visually organize input features into a persistent structure of selected, unselected, and fluctuating categories.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds a specific methodological/result claim about entropy and rank scores and the resulting feature organization that was not in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The generative model based on the mechanistic consumer/resource framework accurately reproduces universal and dataset-specific statistics of bacterial communities.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The generative model based on the consumer/resource framework accurately reproduces universal and dataset-specific statistics of bacterial communities.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this core result about the model reproducing both universal and dataset-specific statistics with essentially identical wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Variation in microbial ecosystem composition arises due to differences in the availability of effective resources while species' resource preferences remain conserved.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model identifies a latent space that represents variables determining the growth and relative abundances of microbial species.
alignments.rationale: The preprint offered a biological conclusion about resource-driven variation and conserved preferences; the publication reframes this as identification of a latent variable space, narrowing the explanatory framing and expressing it with higher stated certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model allows for the analysis of which host phenotypes maximally constrain the composition of host-associated microbiomes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model can predictively capture host-associated microbiomes and the corresponding hosts' phenotypes.
alignments.rationale: The preprint emphasized an analytical capability to identify constraining phenotypes; the publication emphasizes predictive capture of microbiomes and phenotypes, shifting and broadening the claim while increasing asserted certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach aids in the design of microbial communities associated with host phenotypes of interest.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This approach aids in the analysis and design of microbial communities associated with host phenotypes of interest.
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the claim to include both analysis and design (a clarification/broadened scope) but reports this as a conclusion with somewhat lower certainty than the high certainty in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TwinC is an interpretable convolutional neural network model that reliably predicts trans contacts measurable through various genome-wide chromatin conformation assays.
alignments.pub_claim_text: TwinC is an interpretable convolutional neural network model that reliably predicts trans contacts measurable through various genome-wide chromatin conformation assays.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this contribution verbatim, preserving interpretability and predictive reliability claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method achieves high predictive accuracy (AUROC=0.80) on a cross-chromosomal test set from in situ and intact Hi-C experiments in heart tissue.
alignments.pub_claim_text: TwinC achieves high predictive accuracy with an AUROC of 0.80 on a cross-chromosomal test set from in situ and intact Hi-C experiments in heart tissue.
alignments.rationale: The reported performance (AUROC=0.80) and dataset/test details are preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TwinC learns the importance of compartments, chromatin accessibility, clustered transcription factor binding, and G-quadruplexes in forming trans contacts.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The neural network learns the importance of compartments, chromatin accessibility, clustered transcription factor binding, and G-quadruplexes in forming trans contacts.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains these mechanistic/feature-importance conclusions with similar emphasis and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: TwinC provides insights into the underlying principles and functional relevance of trans-genome organization.
alignments.pub_claim_text: TwinC models and interprets trans genome architecture, illuminating this poorly understood aspect of gene regulation.
alignments.rationale: The publication recasts the conclusion to explicitly link trans genome architecture interpretation to gene regulation, broadening the stated functional relevance while keeping a similar level of confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed recurrent neural network (RNN) method for blind source separation effectively transforms input step pulse signals into corresponding ERP difference waveforms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The recurrent neural network (RNN) method for blind source separation effectively transforms input step pulse signals into corresponding ERP difference waveforms.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this result directly, with no substantive change in meaning or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The RNN decomposed ERP difference waveforms into eleven spatially and temporally separate sources that were less noisy and more ERP-specific compared to ICA-derived sources.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The RNN decomposed real ERPs into eleven spatially and temporally separate sources that were less noisy and more ERP-specific than those derived from independent component analysis (ICA).
alignments.rationale: The publication carries over this experimental result with equivalent wording and claims about improvement over ICA.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The RNN sources exhibited less ambiguity between source waveform amplitude, scalp potential polarity, and equivalent current dipole orientation than ICA sources.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The RNN sources exhibited less ambiguity in source waveform amplitude, scalp potential polarity, and equivalent current dipole orientation compared to ICA sources.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this comparative claim about reduced ambiguity with essentially identical content and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed RNN blind source separation method holds promise for further development as a computational model of event-related neural signals.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed RNN blind source separation method holds promise for further development as a computational model of event-related neural signals.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about future promise and model development is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The structural, coding, and biophysical properties of excitatory-inhibitory recurrent networks of spiking neurons emerge from minimizing an instantaneous loss function and a time-averaged performance measure enacting efficient coding.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The efficient coding model is consistent with actual brain cell behavior, organization, and interconnections.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the model conclusion as being consistent with real brain behavior, which frames the original model-emergence claim in a more biological application (broader and somewhat stronger).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The optimal network exhibits biologically-plausible features, including realistic integrate-and-fire spiking dynamics and spike-triggered adaptation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The optimal network derived from efficient coding exhibits biologically plausible features, including realistic integrate-and-fire spiking dynamics and spike-triggered adaptation.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this specific technical result with the same content and level of certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Networks with unstructured connectivity cannot achieve comparable levels of coding efficiency as those with structured connectivity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Networks with unstructured connectivity cannot achieve comparable levels of coding efficiency as those designed under the efficient coding principle.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion is preserved but the publication presents it with slightly reduced certainty; the content remains the same conceptually.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The efficient network solution maintains an instantaneous balance between excitation and inhibition.
alignments.rationale: No publication claim explicitly reports the instantaneous E/I balance finding, so this specific result appears omitted in the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Key properties of biological neural networks may be accounted for by efficient coding.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The efficient coding model is consistent with actual brain cell behavior, organization, and interconnections.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the general conclusion as model consistency with brain data, which clarifies and presents the idea more strongly than the more tentative preprint wording.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The models used in the study are too simple to fully assess the complexity of information processing in the brain.
alignments.rationale: This limitation appears in the publication but was not present among the preprint claims, so it is an added cautionary statement.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Further studies are needed to test more complex mathematical models that better recreate advanced brain activities.
alignments.rationale: The recommendation for future, more complex modeling is a new publication claim not stated in the preprint claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We reveal a mechanism for slow-ramping signals before spontaneous voluntary movements.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Slow ramping signals reflect bounded spontaneous fluctuations that emerge from quasi-winner-take-all dynamics in clustered networks.
alignments.rationale: The publication gives a more specific mechanistic description (quasi-winner-take-all in clustered networks), clarifying and slightly tempering the original broad mechanism claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Slow synapses stabilize spontaneous fluctuations in spiking neural network.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The slow-acting synapses in the network temporally stabilize the dynamics of the clustered networks.
alignments.rationale: The published claim restates the same result about slow synapses stabilizing dynamics, though presented with somewhat more cautious wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We validate model predictions in human frontal cortical single-neuron recordings.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Spontaneous slow ramping activity in single neurons and population activity can be produced by a spiking neural-network model.
alignments.rationale: The preprint asserted empirical validation in human recordings, whereas the publication emphasizes the model's ability to produce such activity; the explicit validation claim appears reduced in the published framing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model recreates the readiness potential in an EEG proxy signal.
alignments.rationale: The publication's listed claims do not mention reproduction of the readiness potential in an EEG proxy, so this specific claim is not present in the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Neurons that ramp together had correlated activity before ramping onset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Neurons that ramp together exhibit correlated firing patterns before ramping onset.
alignments.rationale: This empirical observation is repeated in the publication with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Clair3-RNA is the first deep learning-based variant caller tailored for long-read RNA sequencing data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Clair3-RNA is the first deep learning-based variant caller specifically designed for long-read RNA sequencing data.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the claimed novelty and specific focus of Clair3-RNA unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Clair3-RNA achieved a ~91% SNP F1-score on the ONT platform using the latest ONT SQK-RNA004 kit.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Clair3-RNA achieved a ~91% SNP F1-score on the ONT platform using the latest ONT SQK-RNA004 kit and a ~92% SNP F1-score in PacBio Iso-Seq and MAS-Seq for variants with at least 4x coverage.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the ONT result and expands reported performance to additional platforms and conditions, clarifying and broadening the original performance claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Clair3-RNA consistently outperformed existing callers in extensive evaluation of various GIAB samples.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Clair3-RNA consistently outperformed existing callers and accurately identified RNA editing sites across GIAB samples.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the outperforming result and additionally emphasizes accurate identification of RNA editing sites, strengthening and expanding the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Clair3-RNA is capable of distinguishing RNA high-quality editing sites from variants accurately.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Clair3-RNA consistently outperformed existing callers and accurately identified RNA editing sites across GIAB samples.
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates accurate identification of RNA editing sites within a broader performance summary, making the original capability claim more explicit and strongly stated.

alignments.pub_claim_text: With at least 10x coverage and disregarding zygosity, Clair3-RNA's performance reached a ~95% F1-score for ONT and ~96% for PacBio.
alignments.rationale: This higher-coverage performance breakdown appears in the publication but was not in the preprint claims, so it is an added detailed result.

alignments.pub_claim_text: After phasing, Clair3-RNA's performance reached ~97% for ONT and ~98% for PacBio.
alignments.rationale: The phasing-specific, improved performance metrics are new detailed results in the publication not claimed in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The computational framework developed can identify interactions between domains in immunoglobulin fold proteins with over 70% accuracy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The computational framework developed can identify interactions between domains in the immunoglobulin fold with over 70% accuracy.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this quantitative accuracy claim unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Many interactions recognized by the machine learning classifiers have been experimentally confirmed in the literature.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Many interactions recognized by the machine learning classifiers have been experimentally confirmed in the literature.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same corroboration by literature evidence without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The computational platform serves as a useful tool for identifying potential new interactions between cell surface proteins.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The computational platform serves as a useful tool to help identify potential new interactions between cell surface proteins.
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The general framework of the machine learning classification can be extended to study interactions of proteins in other domain superfamilies.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The general framework of the machine learning classification can be extended to study the interactions of proteins in other domain superfamilies.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the extension claim with the same scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The residual fully-connected neural network (RFCN) demonstrates advantages for modeling genomic profiling data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The residual fully-connected neural network (RFCN) architecture has advantages in modeling genomic profiling data.
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the RFCN advantage claim unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AutoGenome is an end-to-end automated genomic deep learning framework that incorporates AutoML algorithms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: AutoGenome is an end-to-end, automated deep learning framework designed for genomic studies.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the framework contribution with slightly different wording but the same scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AutoGenome can train high-performance deep learning models for various kinds of genomic profiling data automatically.
alignments.pub_claim_text: AutoGenome can automatically train high-performance deep learning models for various kinds of genomic profiling data.
alignments.rationale: The result about automated training of high-performance models is preserved in the publication without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AutoGenome can assess feature importance and export the most important features for supervised learning tasks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: AutoGenome can assess the importance of different features and export the most critical features for supervised learning tasks.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the feature-importance capability with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AutoGenome is envisioned to become a popular tool in genomic studies.
alignments.pub_claim_text: AutoGenome is expected to become a popular tool in genomic studies.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the aspirational prediction about adoption with similar wording and low certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Sound-symbolic mappings rely on domain-specific combinations and weights of acoustic parameters. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Iconic mappings were linked to domain-specific combinations of acoustic parameters. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both claims report the same result linking domain-specific combinations of acoustic parameters to sound-symbolic/iconic mappings with equivalent certainty; wording differs but meaning is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The fast Fourier transform and the fraction of unvoiced frames were present in the best performing model for each meaning domain studied. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: One spectro-temporal parameter, the fast Fourier transform, contributed to all domains, indicating the importance of time-varying spectral properties for iconicity judgments. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes FFT's contribution across domains but omits mention of the fraction of unvoiced frames, so the claim is a narrowed/revised presentation of the preprint result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The predicted and perceptual ratings of the real words were strongly correlated. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The generated shape ratings for 160 real words strongly correlated with perceptual ratings of real words. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same strong correlation but adds a specific sample size and clarifies that the generated ratings were for 160 real words, so the claim is clarified rather than changed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This approach measures the degree of sound-symbolic mapping in natural languages, unconfounded by semantic bias. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication's claims do not reiterate the explicit methodological assertion that the approach measures degree of mapping 'unconfounded by semantic bias', so this preprint claim appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our findings support the proposed relevance of sound symbolism to language. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our findings support the relevance of iconicity to language. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication makes the same conclusion about relevance of iconicity/sound-symbolism but reports it with lower stated certainty, so content is unchanged but confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed machine learning-based method predicts the thermodynamic stability change resulting from tyrosine phosphorylation with a strong correlation to experimental data (R = 0.71). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach strongly correlates with experimental phosphorylation stability and mutational scanning cDNA proteolysis data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both statements report the method's strong correlation with experimental stability data; the publication broadens the types of corroborating data, but the core result is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method predicts destabilizing phosphorylations in both oncogenes and tumor suppressors, indicating a generalized relief of auto-inhibition or activating conformational change in oncogenes. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Destabilizing phosphorylations were predicted in both oncogenes and tumor suppressors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the core predictive result for both oncogenes and tumor suppressors but drops the mechanistic interpretation (relief of auto-inhibition), so the claim is revised and the interpretive confidence is reduced.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The number of circuit topological parallel relations with respect to residues contacting the phosphorylated site is greater for autoinhibited oncogenes than for other proteins (Wilcoxon p = 0.03). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ΔΔG values and local protein circuit topology features can distinguish phospho-proteins that are known to be dysregulated in cancer. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The original specific statistical comparison is reframed in the publication as a general discriminative ability of ΔΔG and topology features to identify cancer-dysregulated phospho-proteins, broadening and slightly tempering the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The top destabilized proteins from the pan-cancer data are enriched for chemical and oxidative stress pathways. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: No publication claim restates the specific pathway enrichment result, so this preprint finding appears omitted in the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach predicts that 58% of recurrent tyrosine cancer phosphorylations are destabilizing at the 1 kcal/mol threshold. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach can enable rapid screening of destabilizing phosphorylations and phosphomimetic mutations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The specific quantitative result (58%) from the preprint is not reported; the publication instead emphasizes the method's utility for rapid screening, reframing the numeric result into an application-oriented conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study introduces a machine learning-based approach for the identification of mRNA signatures specific to non-ulcerative Bladder Pain Syndrome (BPS). (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This study introduces a machine learning (ML)-based approach for the identification of mRNA signatures specific to non-ulcerative Bladder Pain Syndrome (BPS). (certainty=high, type=contribution)
