alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Pan-Multiplex (Pan-M) dataset contains 197 million distinct annotations of marker expression across 15 different cell types. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We constructed the Pan-Multiplex (Pan-M) dataset containing 197 million distinct annotations of marker expression across 15 different cell types. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the dataset construction claim verbatim with equivalent certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Nimbus is a deep learning model that predicts marker positivity from multiplexed image data without requiring any retraining. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Nimbus is a pretrained model that uses the underlying images to classify marker expression of individual cells as positive or negative across distinct cell types, from different tissues, acquired using different microscope platforms, without requiring any retraining. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the description (pretrained, multiple tissues/platforms) but preserves the core claim that Nimbus predicts marker positivity without retraining.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Nimbus predictions capture the underlying staining patterns of the full diversity of markers present in Pan-M. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We demonstrate that Nimbus predictions capture the underlying staining patterns of the full diversity of markers present in Pan-M. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the same result with equivalent framing and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Nimbus predictions can be integrated with downstream clustering algorithms to robustly identify cell subtypes in image data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Nimbus predictions can be integrated with downstream clustering algorithms to robustly identify cell subtypes in image data. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the integration claim but frames it with somewhat lower certainty ('medium' vs 'high'), indicating a tempered confidence.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Nimbus matches or exceeds the accuracy of previous approaches that must be retrained on each dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This explicit claim of parity/ superiority relative to retrained methods does not appear among the listed preprint claims and thus represents an added publication claim emphasizing comparative performance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed computational approach enables the design of a highly diverse library of animal venoms and 'metavenoms'.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We developed an innovative computational approach to design a highly diverse library of animal venoms and 'metavenoms'.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution with only minor wording differences; intent and scope are unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach led to the discovery of Kunitz type domain containing proteins that target the human itch receptor MRGPRX4.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our approach led to the discovery of Kunitz-type domain containing proteins that target the human itch receptor Mas-related G-protein coupled receptor member X4.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same discovery, only expanding the receptor name; meaning and certainty are preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning-based structural homology mining identified two endogenous human homologs, TFPI and SPINT2, which exhibit agonist-dependent potentiation of MRGPRX4.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning-based structural homology mining identified two endogenous human homologs, tissue factor pathway inhibitor (TFPI), and serine peptidase inhibitor, Kunitz type 2 (SPINT2), which exhibit agonist-dependent potentiation of Mas-related G-protein coupled receptor member X4.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result and adds full gene names; core claim and confidence remain the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Highly multiplexed screening of animal venoms and metavenoms is a promising approach to uncover new drug candidates.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Highly multiplexed screening of animal venoms and metavenoms is therefore a promising approach to uncover new drug candidates.
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same conclusion with essentially identical scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Graph-pMHC dramatically outperforms methods with suboptimal inductive biases, such as the multilayer-perceptron-based NetMHCIIan-4.0, with a +22.84% average precision.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Graph-pMHC dramatically outperforms methods with suboptimal inductive biases, such as the multilayer-perceptron-based NetMHCIIpan-4.0, by +20.17% absolute average precision.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a different (smaller) numeric effect and fixes a model name typo, so the result magnitude is revised and the claim is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our strategy achieves a 7.14% ROC AUC improvement in predicting antibody drug immunogenicity compared to BioPhi’s Sapiens score.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model increases receiver operating characteristic curve (ROC)-area under the ROC curve (AUC) by 2.57% compared to just filtering peptides by hits in OASis alone for predicting antibody drug immunogenicity.
alignments.rationale: The publication changes both the comparator and the magnitude (smaller improvement vs a different baseline), so the claim is revised and narrowed with reduced effect size.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Significant progress has been made in pMHCII presentation modeling due to the collection of large-scale pMHC mass spectrometry datasets and advances in deep machine learning.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Significant progress has been made in pMHCII presentation modeling due to the collection of large-scale pMHC mass spectrometry datasets and advances in machine learning.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same contextual conclusion, with only minor wording changes (removing 'deep'), leaving the substance unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MANGEM provides a user-friendly interface for machine-learning alignment methods of neuronal multi-modal data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MANGEM provides a user-friendly interface to machine learning alignment methods for neuronal multimodal data.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution with equivalent wording and intent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MANGEM enables real-time visualization of characteristics of raw and aligned cells.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MANGEM can run asynchronously for large-scale data alignment and offers various downstream analyses and visualizations.
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the statement to include asynchronous operation and broader visualizations; the original visualization capability is retained but clarified with additional details.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MANGEM can be used for large-scale data alignment and offers various downstream analyses of aligned cells.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MANGEM can run asynchronously for large-scale data alignment and offers various downstream analyses and visualizations.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds that MANGEM can run asynchronously and explicitly names visualizations, clarifying but not changing the claim about large-scale alignment and downstream analyses.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The usage of MANGEM was demonstrated by aligning Patch-seq multimodal data of neuronal cells in the mouse visual cortex.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The usage of MANGEM was demonstrated by aligning multimodal data of neuronal cells in the mouse visual cortex.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same demonstration; the minor omission of the 'Patch-seq' label does not change the substance of the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The automated research platform enables continuous, communicative, non-invasive interactions among various sensing and actuation devices.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The automated research platform maintained robust neural activity throughout the experiment while enabling hourly electrophysiology recordings.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the platform description into a specific experimental result (maintenance of activity and hourly recordings), narrowing the original broader capabilities claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The automated protocols were validated in maintaining robust neural activity throughout the 7-day studies of mouse cerebral cortex organoids.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The automated research platform maintained robust neural activity throughout the experiment while enabling hourly electrophysiology recordings.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same validation result (maintenance of robust activity during the experiment) with similar emphasis and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Median neural unit firing rates increased for every sample during the studies.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The median firing rates of neural units increased for each sample during the study.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same quantitative result with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Feeding did not affect firing rate, and performing media exchange during a recording showed no acute effects on firing rate.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Feeding did not affect the firing rate of the organoids.
alignments.rationale: The publication splits the combined preprint observation into separate results; the feeding observation maps directly and is restated without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Feeding did not affect firing rate, and performing media exchange during a recording showed no acute effects on firing rate.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Media exchange during a recording did not show acute effects on firing rate.
alignments.rationale: The publication separately reports the media-exchange observation present in the preprint; this is the same observation presented as its own claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PFOCR is a novel pathway database that approaches the breadth and depth of Gene Ontology.
alignments.pub_claim_text: PFOCR is a novel pathway database that approaches the breadth and depth of Gene Ontology.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution claim verbatim; no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PFOCR content is extracted from published pathway figures at a rate of 1000 new pathways each month.
alignments.rationale: The numerical extraction-rate claim from the preprint does not appear in the publication and is therefore treated as removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PFOCR provides unique advantages in cancer subtype and grade prediction analyses compared to popular pathway databases.
alignments.pub_claim_text: PFOCR demonstrates unique advantages in cancer subtype and grade prediction analyses compared to popular pathway databases.
alignments.rationale: The publication elevates the claim from a conclusion to a stated result and increases certainty, so the claim is clarified and confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning-based protein design tools can be used to design small proteins that arc above the peptide binding groove of pMHC complexes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We used RFdiffusion to design pMHCI-binding proteins that make extensive contacts with the peptide.
alignments.rationale: Both claims report designing small proteins that engage the pMHC peptide region; the publication reframes the geometry as making extensive peptide contacts, which clarifies rather than contradicts the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Specific binders for ten target pMHCs were identified, which bind the on-target pMHC tetramer but not closely related peptides.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We identified specific binders for 11 target pMHCIs starting from either experimental or predicted pMHCI structures.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a different numerical result (11 vs 10) and explicitly notes starting-structure sources, so the claim is a revised (and somewhat broadened) version of the preprint result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Incorporation of designs into chimeric antigen receptors leads to T-cell activation by cognate pMHC complexes well above background levels.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Designs for eight targets conferred peptide-specific T cell activation upon incorporation into chimeric antigen receptors.
alignments.rationale: Both claims report CAR-mediated, peptide-specific T cell activation, but the publication specifies results for eight targets (a narrower, quantified outcome) rather than the broader phrasing in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach can generate high specificity binders starting from either experimental or predicted structures of the target pMHC complexes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We identified specific binders for 11 target pMHCIs starting from either experimental or predicted pMHCI structures.
alignments.rationale: The publication provides stronger, higher-confidence evidence (more targets and explicit identification) that starting from experimental or predicted structures yields specific binders, amplifying and clarifying the preprint's conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep-learning framework ROSIE can computationally impute the expression and localization of dozens of proteins from H&E images.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ROSIE is a deep-learning framework that computationally imputes the expression and localization of dozens of proteins from H&E images.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the framework's core contribution verbatim; there is no substantive change in meaning or scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Validation of the in silico mIF staining method on held-out H&E samples demonstrates that the predicted biomarkers are effective in identifying cell phenotypes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Validation of the in silico mIF staining method on held-out H&E samples demonstrates that the predicted biomarkers are effective in identifying cell phenotypes.
alignments.rationale: The validation result appears unchanged between the preprint and publication, with identical scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ROSIE facilitates the robust identification of stromal and epithelial microenvironments and immune cell subtypes like tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ROSIE facilitates the robust identification of stromal and epithelial microenvironments and immune cell subtypes like tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes (TILs).
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same conclusion, adding only the TILs abbreviation; the substantive claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The multi-session reduced-rank regression model improves decoding accuracy by sharing behaviorally-relevant statistical structure in neural activity across sessions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our models provide low-dimensional neural representations, task-related single-neuron contributions, and brain-wide timescales of neural activation.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the low-dimensional representations produced by the models, which operationalizes the preprint's claim about shared structure improving decoding, though the mechanism wording is more representation-focused.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The multi-session state-space model enhances decoding by sharing similar behavioral statistical structure across trials and sessions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our models provide low-dimensional neural representations, task-related single-neuron contributions, and brain-wide timescales of neural activation.
alignments.rationale: The publication consolidates methodological claims into a description of the model outputs (low-dimensional representations and neuron contributions), which clarifies but does not contradict the preprint's multi-session/state-space mechanism.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our decoders demonstrate improved decoding accuracy for four distinct behaviors compared to traditional approaches.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our decoders outperform traditional approaches on four behaviors.
alignments.rationale: Both statements report the same quantitative performance result across four behaviors; wording is effectively identical in meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results of our models generalize across additional datasets, species, and behavioral tasks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results generalize across datasets, species, and tasks.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same generalization claim with higher certainty; content is unchanged but confidence is amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Unlike existing deep learning approaches, our models are interpretable and efficient, uncovering low-dimensional representations that predict animal decisions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Unlike deep learning methods, our models are efficient and interpretable.
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the same comparative claim (efficiency and interpretability) more firmly; the publication also emphasizes these attributes as a conclusion with higher certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: VISTA is a novel approach designed to predict the expression levels of unobserved genes specifically tailored for subcellular spatial transcriptomics data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: VISTA predicts unmeasured gene expression in SST data by integrating scRNA-seq and SST through variational inference and geometric deep learning with uncertainty quantification.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the core contribution but adds methodological detail (integration approach) and uncertainty quantification, which clarifies the preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: VISTA jointly models single-cell RNA-seq data and subcellular spatial transcriptomics data based on variational inference and geometric deep learning.
alignments.pub_claim_text: VISTA predicts unmeasured gene expression in SST data by integrating scRNA-seq and SST through variational inference and geometric deep learning with uncertainty quantification.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the joint modeling approach using the same methods and adds uncertainty quantification; this is a direct match to the preprint's methodological claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using four subcellular spatial transcriptomics datasets, VISTA demonstrates superior performance in imputation and in analyzing large-scale datasets with satisfactory time efficiency and memory consumption.
alignments.pub_claim_text: VISTA achieves superior imputation accuracy, scalability, and efficiency across four datasets.
alignments.rationale: Both claims assert superior imputation performance and scalability/efficiency across the same four datasets; the publication condenses wording but keeps the claim intact.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The imputation of VISTA enables the detection of new spatially variable genes and the discovery of novel ligand-receptor interactions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The accurate imputation provided by VISTA supports diverse downstream analyses, including identifying spatially variable genes and uncovering ligand-receptor interactions.
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly lists the same downstream analyses (spatially variable genes, ligand–receptor interactions), matching the preprint claim closely.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: VISTA allows for the inference of spatial RNA velocity and improved decomposition of spatial and intrinsic variations.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report or highlight the specific capabilities for spatial RNA velocity inference or the stated decomposition improvements, so this specific claim appears omitted in the published version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A population of cingulate neurons encodes spontaneous pain-related behaviors and is selectively modulated by morphine.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A population of cingulate neurons encodes spontaneous pain-related behaviours and is selectively modulated by morphine.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this core experimental finding with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Morphine reversed neuropathic neural dynamics and reduced affective-motivational behaviors without altering sensory detection or reflexive responses.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Morphine reversed neuropathic neural dynamics and reduced affective–motivational behaviours without altering sensory detection or reflexive responses.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this result verbatim in substance and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The opioid-mimetic gene therapy recapitulated the analgesic effects of morphine during chronic neuropathic pain.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The opioid-mimetic chemogenetic gene therapy recapitulated the analgesic effects of morphine during chronic neuropathic pain.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same experimental result; wording is effectively the same (chemogenetic detail does not change the claim's meaning).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings offer a new strategy for precision pain management targeting a key nociceptive cortical opioid circuit with safe, on-demand analgesia.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings offer a new strategy for precision pain management that targets a key nociceptive cortical opioid circuit with safe, on-demand analgesia.
alignments.rationale: The concluding translational claim is preserved in the publication with the same scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning-based tool ProteinMPNN can be used to redesign Fe(II)/αKG superfamily enzymes for greater stability, solubility, and expression while retaining both native activity and industrially-relevant non-native functions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep learning-based tool ProteinMPNN can redesign Fe(II)/αKG superfamily enzymes for greater stability, solubility, and expression while retaining both native activity and industrially relevant non-native functions.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same claim with essentially identical scope and confidence about ProteinMPNN redesign of Fe(II)/αKG enzymes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Variants obtained from the stabilized scaffold showed substantially larger increases in non-native activity compared to those from the wild-type enzyme.
alignments.pub_claim_text: After three rounds of directed evolution, a 6-fold activity increase from the wild-type parent and an 80-fold increase from the stabilized variant were obtained.
alignments.rationale: The publication provides quantitative evolution outcomes that substantiate and clarify the preprint's qualitative statement that stabilized-start variants achieved much larger activity increases than wild-type.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Stabilization by computational sequence redesign could be routinely implemented as a first step in directed evolution campaigns for novel biocatalysts.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results provide a framework for the routine implementation of deep learning-based protein stabilization tools in directed evolution workflows for novel biocatalysts.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the preprint's proposal as a provided framework for routine implementation, keeping the same thrust and confidence level.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Tissue compaction is necessary for the appearance of scutoids in the embryo.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Tissue compaction is necessary for the appearance of scutoids in the sea star embryo.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the organism (sea star), narrowing the claim from a generic embryo to a particular model while preserving the finding and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Cell density promotes scutoid formation at a global level.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Cell density promotes scutoid formation at a global level.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this result verbatim with the same scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The increase in the proportion of scutoids is directly associated with cell divisions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The increase in the proportion of scutoids is directly associated with cell divisions.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this conclusion unchanged in wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Apico-basal intercalations appearing just after mitosis may help accommodate new cells within the tissue.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Apico-basal intercalations appearing immediately after mitosis may help accommodate new cells within the tissue.
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same low-confidence conclusion with only a minor wording change ('just' -> 'immediately').

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MAGMA-seq is an integrated technology that combines multiple antigens and multiple antibodies to determine quantitative biophysical parameters using deep sequencing.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MAGMA-seq is an integrated technology that combines multiple antigens and multiple antibodies to determine quantitative biophysical parameters using deep sequencing.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the contribution claim identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MAGMA-seq enables the comprehensive mapping of potential antibody development pathways and sequence-binding relationships for multiple antibodies simultaneously.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MAGMA-seq enables the comprehensive mapping of potential antibody development pathways and sequence-binding relationships for multiple antibodies simultaneously.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this result verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MAGMA-seq allows for the identification of paratope sequence determinants for binding recognition for broadly neutralizing antibodies (bnAbs).
alignments.pub_claim_text: MAGMA-seq allows for the identification of paratope sequence determinants for binding recognition for broadly neutralizing antibodies (bnAbs).
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this specific result unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MAGMA-seq facilitates rapid and scalable antibody engineering of multiple lead candidates by measuring binding for mutants of many given parental antibodies in a single experiment.
alignments.pub_claim_text: MAGMA-seq facilitates rapid and scalable antibody engineering of multiple lead candidates by measuring binding for mutants of many given parental antibodies in a single experiment.
alignments.rationale: The publication carries over this conclusion identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Neuropathological examination revealed classical forms of tau in the hippocampal formation, including extensive Pick bodies and modest numbers of neurofibrillary tangles and dystrophic neurites.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The microscopically diffuse tau in the neuropil in adjacent CA4 and hilus regions exhibit far greater density of fibrillar signal.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes specific regional and fibrillation-density observations in the hippocampus; this is a narrower, more focused formulation of the broader neuropathological description in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: µXRD indicated that Pick bodies are low in fibril content, whereas neurofibrillary lesions within adjacent tissue exhibit far greater density of fibrillar tau.
alignments.pub_claim_text: μXRD data indicate that Pick bodies of the granular layer are relatively low in fibril content.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same μXRD observation about Pick bodies being relatively low in fibril content.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: μXRF demonstrated elevated levels of zinc, calcium, and phosphorous in all tau-containing lesions, whereas sulfur deposition was greatest in lesions exhibiting high fibrillar content.
alignments.pub_claim_text: μXRF data show elevated levels of zinc, calcium and phosphorous relative to surrounding tissue in essentially all tau-containing lesions.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same elemental-elevation finding but omits the specific sulfur-fibril content coupling in this claim, providing a slightly streamlined statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Correlation of lesion morphology with anatomical localization, tau fibrillation, and differential elemental accumulation suggests tau fibrils generate biochemically distinct microenvironments that influence lesion morphology, tau seed formation, and spreading.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Lesions containing different levels of tau fibrils harbor biochemically distinct microenvironments.
alignments.rationale: The publication extracts and emphasizes the core conclusion about distinct microenvironments associated with differing tau-fibril levels; the broader mechanistic and spreading implications from the preprint are presented more succinctly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Weakly electric fish can determine the resistance and capacitance of an object despite small and highly-dependent field distortions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using artificial datasets, we trained an artificial neural network to extract the 3D location, size, and electrical properties of objects.
alignments.rationale: The publication demonstrates an ANN can extract electrical properties from datasets (supporting the preprint claim), but it presents modeled/ANN evidence rather than a direct behavioral demonstration that fish determine resistance/capacitance, so the claim is presented more cautiously.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model developed provides an accurate and efficient method for generating large artificial data sets simulating fish interacting with various objects.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We constructed a filter-based model that accurately accounts for the responses of electroreceptor afferents.
alignments.rationale: The publication's filter-based model that accounts for afferent responses corresponds to the preprint's model used to generate realistic artificial datasets; the functional claim is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The artificial neural network (ANN) trained on these data sets performs best when operating in two stages: first estimating object distance and size, then extracting electrical properties.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results suggest a specific form of modularity in electrosensory processing that can be tested experimentally.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the two-stage/two-module finding as evidence for testable modularity in processing; this is a more conceptual articulation of the preprint's two-stage ANN performance result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This suggests a specific form of modularity in the electrosensory system that can be tested experimentally.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results suggest a specific form of modularity in electrosensory processing that can be tested experimentally.
alignments.rationale: The publication directly repeats this conceptual conclusion about modularity with the same emphasis and testable framing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study highlights the potential of end-to-end modeling for studies of sensory processing.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of the artificial neural network is comparable to that of real fish in experimentally tested behavioral tasks.
alignments.rationale: The publication provides empirical support (ANN performance comparable to fish) that substantiates and sharpens the preprint's broader claim about the value of end-to-end modeling for sensory processing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The classification capacity of a neuron equipped with nonlinear parallel synapses substantially exceeds that of the Perceptron. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The classification capacity of a neuron equipped with nonlinear parallel synapses substantially exceeds that of the Perceptron. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this result verbatim with the same certainty and framing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The number of correctly classified data points can increase superlinearly as the number of presynaptic axons grows. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The number of correctly classified data points can increase superlinearly as the number of presynaptic axons grows. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication contains the same claim with matching wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Successful learning in the model neuron often requires only a small number of parallel synapses. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Successful learning in the model neuron often requires only a small number of parallel synapses. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion appears unchanged in the published version with the same emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Multiple nonlinear synapses per input axon can substantially enhance a neuron's computational power. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Multiple nonlinear synapses per input axon can substantially enhance a neuron's computational power. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this high-certainty conclusion with equivalent wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The application of parallel synapses in a feedforward neural network can increase the test accuracy for classifying MNIST images. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This specific result about MNIST test accuracy does not appear in the publication's listed claims and thus appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Architectural inductive biases have a prominent role in the emergence of cortex-aligned representations in wide networks with minimal training. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Architectural inductive biases have a prominent role in the emergence of cortex-aligned representations in wide networks with minimal training. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this claim verbatim with unchanged certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Cortex-aligned representations emerge in convolutional architectures that combine compression in the spatial domain and expansion in the feature domain. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Cortex-aligned representations emerge in convolutional architectures that combine compression in the spatial domain and expansion in the feature domain. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The inductive biases of convolutional architectures are critical for obtaining performance gains from feature expansion. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The inductive biases of convolutional architectures are critical for obtaining performance gains from feature expansion. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion and its certainty are carried over unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Dimensionality manipulations were relatively ineffective in other architectures and in convolutional models with targeted lesions. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Dimensionality manipulations were relatively ineffective in other architectures and in convolutional models with targeted lesions. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is restated in the publication with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The architectural constraints of convolutional networks allow many aspects of cortical visual representation to emerge even before synaptic connections have been tuned through experience. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The architectural constraints of convolutional networks allow many aspects of cortical visual representation to emerge even before synaptic connections have been tuned through experience. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this low-certainty conceptual conclusion without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed 3D kinematic model generates realistic simulated walking that resembles real fly walking kinematics. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed 3D kinematic model generates realistic simulated walking that resembles real fly walking kinematics. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this core result with the same framing and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model sustains walking even when subjected to unexpected perturbations, generalizing beyond its training data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model sustains walking even when subjected to unexpected perturbations, generalizing beyond its training data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this generalization result verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model's robustness to perturbations deteriorates when sensorimotor delay parameters exceed the physiological range. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model's robustness to perturbations deteriorates when sensorimotor delay parameters exceed the physiological range. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: DistPepFold outperforms AFM and other existing peptide docking methods based on evaluations on two data sets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the outperforming-on-two-datasets claim but expands its scope to include additional peptide docking methods beyond AFM.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The student model in DistPepFold was able to learn from the teacher model to make structural improvements based on AlphaFold-Multimer predictions. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: DistPepFold leverages a teacher model trained with native interaction information to enhance its performance. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the same teacher-student idea more generally (teacher trained with native interactions enhances performance), clarifying without contradicting the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CAZyLingua is the first tool that uses protein language model embeddings to facilitate the annotation of carbohydrate-active enzymes (CAZymes) in metagenomic datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CAZyLingua is a powerful tool that effectively augments existing functional annotation pipelines for CAZymes.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the contribution claim that CAZyLingua augments annotation pipelines but omits the explicit novelty claim of being the 'first' and the explicit mention of language-model embeddings, so the contribution is clarified rather than unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Benchmarking results showed that CAZyLingua achieved a higher F1 score on annotated genomes compared to the traditional sequence homology-based method in dbCAN2.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CAZyLingua demonstrated high performance, maintaining precision and recall comparable to state-of-the-art hidden Markov model-based methods while outperforming purely sequence-based approaches.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the benchmarking in broader terms (HMM vs sequence-based approaches) rather than a single dbCAN2 comparison, expanding the scope while preserving the performance claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CAZyLingua revealed unannotated CAZymes linked to microbial development during infancy when applied to a paired mother/infant longitudinal dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CAZyLingua identified over 27,000 putative CAZymes missed by other tools, including horizontally-transferred enzymes implicated in infant microbiome development.
alignments.rationale: The publication states the same discovery and gives a quantified result and additional detail (27,000 enzymes and horizonal transfer), which broadens but does not contradict the preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CAZyLingua uncovered CAZymes associated with disease and healthy states in metagenomic datasets derived from patients with fibrosis-prone diseases.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CAZyLingua uncovered disease-associated CAZymes, highlighting an expansion of carbohydrate esterases (CEs) in IgG4-related disease.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies a particular disease (IgG4-related disease) and a family-level expansion (CEs), narrowing the broader disease-associated claim from the preprint while clarifying the specific finding.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CAZyLingua discovered new annotations that were previously overlooked by traditional sequence homology tools.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CAZyLingua identified over 27,000 putative CAZymes missed by other tools, including horizontally-transferred enzymes implicated in infant microbiome development.
alignments.rationale: The publication provides the concrete evidence (27,000 putative CAZymes missed by other tools) that supports the preprint's claim about discovering annotations overlooked by homology-based methods.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning framework AkitaR improves predictions of genome folding by leveraging genome sequences and genome-wide RNA-DNA interactions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep learning framework AkitaR improves predictions of genome folding by leveraging both genome sequences and genome-wide RNA-DNA interactions.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same core result with equivalent wording and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Both nascent transcripts and trans-located caRNAs enhance model predictions, particularly in cell-type-specific genomic regions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Both nascent transcripts and trans-located caRNAs enhance model predictions, particularly in cell-type-specific genomic regions.
alignments.rationale: The claim is carried over verbatim with unchanged scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The analysis revealed that caRNAs contribute to the models' predictions at TAD boundaries, chromatin loops, and nuclear sub-structures.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The analysis reveals that caRNAs contribute to the models’ predictions at TAD boundaries, chromatin loops, and nuclear sub-structures.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same finding with equivalent phrasing and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Several novel RNAs, including RNY5, RPPH1, POLG-DT, and THBS1-IT, may modulate chromatin architecture through trans-interactions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Several new non-coding RNAs, including RNY5, RPPH1, POLG-DT, and THBS1-IT1, may modulate chromatin architecture through trans-interactions.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same biological conclusion but slightly revises the annotation of THBS1-IT to THBS1-IT1, a minor clarification of the implicated RNA identity.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Transcripts from Alus and other repetitive elements may facilitate chromatin interactions through trans R-loop formation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Transcripts from Alus and other repetitive elements may facilitate chromatin interactions through trans R-loop formation.
alignments.rationale: The claim text is the same but the publication lowers the certainty (publication labels the conclusion as low confidence), so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The first CASP exercise that involved RNA structure modeling assessed forty-two predictor groups for twelve RNA-containing targets.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not repeat the explicit claim about this being the 'first' CASP exercise involving RNA modeling nor the specific count of groups/targets, so this preprint-specific contextual claim was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The top-ranked predictor groups in the assessments were AIchemy_RNA2, Chen, and RNAPolis and GeneSilico (tied), with deep learning approaches performing significantly worse.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The assessments of RNA structure predictions in CASP15 ranked AIchemy_RNA2, Chen, and RNAPolis as the top three predictor groups.
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same ranking of top predictor groups; the preprint's note about deep learning performing worse is addressed separately in another publication claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Models submitted by CASP15 groups correctly predicted the global fold of the RNA targets, except for two RNA-protein complexes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Models submitted by CASP15 groups correctly predicted the global fold of the RNA targets, with the exception of two RNA-protein complexes.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this result verbatim, preserving scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Current RNA modeling approaches show potential utility for RNA nanotechnology and structural biology.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Current RNA modeling approaches have potential utility for RNA nanotechnology and structural biology.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same conclusion about potential utility with equivalent wording and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Challenges remain in modeling fine details such as non-canonical pairs and in predicting multiple structures resolved by cryo-EM or crystallography.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Challenges remain in modeling fine details such as noncanonical pairs and in ranking among submitted models.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the limitation about modeling fine details but replaces the specific issue of predicting multiple resolved structures with a different emphasis on ranking among models, a substantive revision and narrowing of the original limitation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CoVar identifies key genes not found through independent differential expression analysis.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CoVar captures the intrinsic variationality and modularity in expression data, identifying key driver genes not found through existing differential analysis methodologies.
alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same result that CoVar finds key driver genes missed by differential analysis, using slightly broader language but retaining the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CoVar leverages ML-based network inference to find networks that capture the strength of regulatory interactions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CoVar is an ML-based framework that identifies central genes driving perturbed gene expression across biological states.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames CoVar at the framework/result level (identifying central genes) rather than the specific methodological phrasing about network inference capturing interaction strength, so the method is presented in clarified, higher-level terms.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model pinpoints a subset of genes, termed variational, whose expression variabilities typify the differences in network connectivity between control and perturbed data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CoVar focuses on identifying variational genes that undergo changes in their expression network interaction profiles.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same concept of 'variational' genes and their changing network interaction profiles, matching the preprint definition and intent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Variational genes capture gene expression variability by being differentially expressed themselves or possessing differentially expressed neighbor genes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CoVar captures the intrinsic variationality and modularity in expression data, identifying key driver genes not found through existing differential analysis methodologies.
alignments.rationale: The publication generalizes the mechanism-focused preprint statement into a broader description of capturing intrinsic variationality and modularity; the mechanistic detail is deemphasized but consistent with the higher-level claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Cryo2Struct built substantially more accurate and complete protein structural models than the widely used ab initio method - Phenix.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Cryo2Struct produces substantially more accurate and complete protein structural models than the widely used ab initio method Phenix.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same comparative performance claim with equivalent wording and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Cryo2Struct demonstrated improved performance on a new test dataset of 500 recently released density maps compared to the standard dataset.
alignments.rationale: The specific preprint claim about improved performance on a particular 500-map test dataset is not present in the publication, indicating that this dataset-specific result was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of Cryo2Struct is robust against changes in the resolution of density maps and the size of protein structures.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Cryo2Struct's performance in building atomic structural models is robust against changes in the resolution of density maps and the size of protein structures.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the robustness claim about resolution and protein size with equivalent phrasing and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our findings reinforce the generalization of binary classifiers for Crohn's disease (CD) and colorectal cancer (CRC) to hold-out cohorts. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our findings reinforce the generalization of binary classifiers for Crohn's disease (CD) and colorectal cancer (CRC) to hold-out cohorts. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this result verbatim with the same certainty and scope, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We identified high microbial similarity in disease pairs like CD vs ulcerative colitis (UC), CD vs CRC, Parkinson's disease vs type 2 diabetes (T2D), and schizophrenia vs T2D. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We identified high microbial similarity in disease pairs like CD vs ulcerative colitis (UC), CD vs CRC, Parkinson's disease vs type 2 diabetes (T2D), and schizophrenia vs T2D. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this result with the same examples and certainty, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We found strong inverse correlations in Alzheimer's disease vs CD and UC. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We found strong inverse correlations in Alzheimer's disease vs CD and UC. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This result appears identically in the publication with the same level of certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These findings detected by our pipeline provide valuable insights into these diseases. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our study provides a modest first step in underscoring the potential of integrating microbiome insights into the disease similarity assessment. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the broad "valuable insights" conclusion as a more modest "first step" emphasizing potential, which clarifies and tones down the original interpretation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our defined loss function outperforms loss functions commonly used in contrastive learning. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our defined loss function outperforms loss functions commonly used in contrastive learning. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this claim verbatim and with the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our method displays good preservation of global structure and has improved generalization properties over t-SNE, UMAP, and popvae. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method displays good preservation of global structure and has improved generalization properties over t-distributed stochastic neighbor embedding, Uniform Manifold Approximation and Projection, and popvae. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same comparison and conclusion (with expanded acronyms) with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The deep learning framework allows for projecting new samples and fine-tuning to new datasets using a pre-trained model without access to the original training data. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The deep learning framework allows for projecting new samples and fine-tuning to new datasets using a pretrained model without access to the original training data. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This capability is stated identically in the publication with the same level of certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our method preserves relative distances between individuals to a high extent. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method preserves relative distances between individuals to a high extent. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this empirical result with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We provide examples of population classification on two datasets of dog and human genotypes. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We provide examples of population classification on two datasets of dog and human genotypes. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The examples and their framing are preserved in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SeqDance predicted dynamic property changes reflect mutation effect on protein folding stability. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SeqDance predicted dynamic property changes reflect mutation effect on protein folding stability. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This result is present verbatim in the publication with matching certainty and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ESMDance substantially outperforms ESM2 in zero-shot prediction of mutation effects for designed and viral proteins which lack evolutionary information. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ESMDance substantially outperforms ESM2 in zero-shot prediction of mutation effects for designed and viral proteins which lack evolutionary information. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents this comparative performance claim unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SeqDance and ESMDance offer a new framework for integrating protein dynamics into language models. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SeqDance and ESMDance are two protein language models trained on dynamic biophysical properties derived from molecular dynamics simulations and normal mode analyses of over 64,000 proteins. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication expands this contribution claim with concrete training details (data sources and scale), clarifying the framework rather than contradicting it.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The linguistic embedding space can capture the linguistic content of word-by-word neural alignment between speaker and listener. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The contextual embeddings better capture word-by-word neural alignment between speaker and listener than syntactic and articulatory models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the core claim but makes it comparative and thus stronger (shows embeddings perform better than alternatives), which clarifies and amplifies the original statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Linguistic content emerged in the speaker's brain before word articulation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Linguistic content emerges in the speaker's brain before word articulation and rapidly re-emerges in the listener's brain after word articulation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication combines the two temporal findings into a single statement but preserves the original speaker-before-articulation claim without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The same linguistic content rapidly reemerged in the listener's brain after word articulation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Linguistic content emerges in the speaker's brain before word articulation and rapidly re-emerges in the listener's brain after word articulation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication includes this listener re-emergence result together with the speaker result, preserving the original finding.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These findings establish a computational framework to study how human brains transmit their thoughts to one another in real-world contexts. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The contextual embeddings learned by LLMs can serve as an explicit numerical model of the shared, context-rich meaning space humans use to communicate their thoughts to one another. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the broad "computational framework" claim to a more specific statement (LLM contextual embeddings as an explicit numerical model) and lowers certainty, which clarifies and tempers the original conclusion.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The BEPH model leverages self-supervised learning to learn meaningful representations from 11 million unlabeled histopathological images. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: BEPH is a foundation model that leverages self-supervised learning to learn meaningful representations from 11 million unlabeled histopathological images. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this contribution claim with equivalent scope and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental results demonstrate that the BEPH model consistently outperforms several comparative models with similar parameters, even with limited training data reduced to 50%. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: BEPH can be efficiently adapted to various tasks, including patch-level cancer diagnosis, WSI-level cancer classification, and survival prediction for multiple cancer subtypes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes task adaptability rather than the preprint's specific performance claims under reduced-data regimes; the performance claim is reframed and de-emphasized.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The BEPH model can improve ResNet and DINO by up to a maximum increase of 8.8% and 7.2% in WSI level classification, and 6.44% and 3.28% on average in survival prediction, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report the specific numeric performance improvements versus ResNet and DINO present in the preprint, so this detailed numerical claim appears removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BEPH offers a universal solution to enhance model performance, reduce the burden of expert annotations, and enable widespread clinical applications of artificial intelligence. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The masked image modeling (MIM) pre-training approach used in BEPH enhances model performance and reduces reliance on expert annotations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the core idea (enhanced performance and reduced annotation burden) but attributes it to a specific MIM pre-training approach and frames it more cautiously, clarifying and tempering the original broad claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ADMET-AI provides fast and accurate ADMET predictions as both a website and a Python package. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ADMET-AI is a machine learning platform that provides fast and accurate ADMET predictions. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution but omits the specific delivery formats (website and Python package), a minor wording change that clarifies rather than revises the core claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ADMET-AI has the highest average rank on the TDC ADMET Benchmark Group leaderboard. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ADMET-AI has the highest average rank on the TDC ADMET Leaderboard. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same performance claim with effectively identical meaning and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ADMET-AI is currently the fastest web-based ADMET predictor, with a 45% reduction in time compared to the next fastest ADMET web server. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ADMET-AI is currently the fastest web-based ADMET predictor, with a 45% reduction in time compared to the next fastest public ADMET web server. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication adds the word 'public' to specify the comparator but preserves the same quantitative performance claim; this is a minor clarification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ADMET-AI can predict ADMET properties for one million molecules in just 3.1 hours when run locally. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ADMET-AI can run predictions for one million molecules in just 3.1 hours. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same throughput figure but omits the explicit 'when run locally' qualifier, a small wording change that clarifies the reported capability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We have developed a machine learning approach that prioritizes interactions by analyzing quantitative data from binary PPI assays and AlphaFold-Multimer predictions. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We have developed a machine learning approach that prioritizes interactions by analyzing quantitative data from binary PPI assays or AlphaFold-Multimer predictions. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication changes the connector from 'and' to 'or', shifting applicability from requiring both data types to allowing either, which revises and broadens the claim's applicability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using the quantitative assay LuTHy together with our machine learning algorithm, we identified high-confidence interactions among SARS-CoV-2 proteins. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using the quantitative assay LuTHy together with our machine learning algorithm, we identified high-confidence interactions among SARS-CoV-2 proteins. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is restated identically in the publication with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We identified a compound that binds to NSP10 and inhibits its interaction with NSP16, while also disrupting the methyltransferase activity of the complex, and SARS-CoV-2 replication. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We identified a compound that binds to NSP10 and inhibits its interaction with NSP16, while also disrupting the methyltransferase activity of the complex, and SARS-CoV-2 replication. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same experimental and antiviral result without modification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This pipeline will help to prioritize PPI targets to accelerate the discovery of early-stage drug candidates targeting protein complexes and pathways. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This pipeline will help to prioritize PPI targets to accelerate the discovery of early-stage drug candidates targeting protein complexes and pathways. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about the pipeline's utility is carried over verbatim into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Task-relevant eye movements reflect an embodiment of the subjects' dynamically evolving internal beliefs about the goal. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Eye movements constitute a vital cognitive resource to support neural computations for memory-guided navigation. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the preprint's idea more generally as eye movements being a cognitive resource for memory-guided navigation, which reframes and clarifies the original claim about reflecting internal beliefs.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A neural network model with tuned recurrent connectivity between oculomotor and evidence-integrating frontoparietal circuits accounted for the behavioral strategy observed in the task. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Participants physically track the latent target position with their gaze even in the absence of optic flow. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint's claim about a model accounting for behavioral strategy is reported in the publication as empirical participant tracking; the mapping links the modeled behavioral account to the observed behavior, but the focus shifts between model and empirical description.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The neural network model better explained neural data from monkeys' posterior parietal cortex compared to task-optimized models unconstrained by an oculomotor-based cognitive strategy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed neural network model better explained neural data from male monkeys’ posterior parietal cortex compared to models optimized solely for task performance. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both statements report the same model comparison result with equivalent scope and meaning; wording differences are minor.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: These results highlight the importance of unconstrained movement in working memory computations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: These results highlight the importance of eye movements in working memory computations. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication replaces 'unconstrained movement' with the more specific 'eye movements', clarifying the target of the conclusion while preserving its intent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study establishes a functional significance of oculomotor signals for evidence-integration and navigation computations via embodied cognition. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study establishes a functional significance of oculomotor signals for evidence-integration and navigation computations via embodied cognition. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication carries over the same conclusion about oculomotor signals and embodied cognition without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Infants whose parents provide more bids for joint attention display a more complex neural response when viewing social scenes. (certainty=medium, type=hypothesis)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Infants whose parents provide more bids for joint attention display a more complex neural response when viewing social scenes. (certainty=medium, type=hypothesis)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same hypothesized association without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a significant positive association between parent alternating gaze and neural entropy in frontal and central brain regions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a significant positive association between parent alternating gaze and neural entropy in frontal and central regions of infants. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The reported significant association is preserved in the publication with only minor wording changes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results suggest a relationship between parent behavior and infant neural mechanisms that regulate social attention. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results suggest a relationship between parent behavior and infant neural mechanisms that regulate social attention. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about parent behavior and infant neural mechanisms is restated identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Parent cues are important in the formation of neural networks in infancy. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Parental cues are important in forming neural networks in infants. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same conclusion about the role of parental cues with only minor rephrasing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning approaches can significantly speed up the analysis of electron microscopy images of tumor biopsies. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning approaches can significantly speed up the analysis of electron microscopy images of tumor biopsies. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same high-level conclusion without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study demonstrates improvements in 3D Dice score when using state-of-the-art deep learning models for segmenting nuclei and nucleoli. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study demonstrates improvements in 3D Dice score when using state-of-the-art deep learning models for segmenting nuclei and nucleoli. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The reported performance improvements are carried over verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Utilizing unlabeled images through semi-supervised learning with Cross Pseudo Supervision contributes to the analysis of tumor biopsy images. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Utilizing unlabeled images through semi-supervised learning with Cross Pseudo Supervision contributes to the effectiveness of the segmentation models. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the contribution in terms of improving segmentation model effectiveness rather than the broader 'analysis of tumor biopsy images', a focused clarification of the claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The analysis indicates that more complex models provide relative gains in segmentation performance. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The analysis provides insights into the relative gains of using more complex models for segmentation tasks. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same conclusion with slightly different wording emphasizing the insights into relative gains; the substantive claim remains the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A new, integrated approach was developed to analyze the sequence, modification, and aminoacylation state of tRNA molecules.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The aa-tRNA-seq method reveals the identity of the amino acids attached to all tRNAs in a cellular sample at the single molecule level.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies a concrete readout (amino acid identity at single-molecule level), which refines the broader preprint claim about analyzing sequence/modification/aminoacylation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method leverages a chemical ligation that embeds the charged amino acid in an adapted tRNA molecule.
alignments.rationale: The publication claims do not mention the specific chemical ligation/embedding approach as a claim, so this procedural detail appears omitted from the highlighted publication claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: During nanopore sequencing, the embedded amino acid generates unique distortions in ionic current and translocation speed.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning models enable the accurate identification of amino acid identities based on unique signal distortions generated by interactions with the nanopore.
alignments.rationale: Both claims describe unique nanopore signal distortions from amino acids; the publication emphasizes ML-based identification, clarifying how those distortions are used.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method indicates it will be broadly useful for examining relationships and dependencies between tRNA sequence, modification, and aminoacylation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The studies lay the groundwork for understanding the efficiency and fidelity of tRNA aminoacylation as a function of tRNA sequence, modification, and environmental conditions.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies particular downstream questions (efficiency/fidelity, environmental conditions), narrowing the preprint's broader claim about general utility.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Carafe generates high-quality experiment-specific in silico spectral libraries by training deep learning models directly on DIA data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Carafe generates high-quality experiment-specific in silico spectral libraries by training deep learning models directly on DIA data.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the preprint contribution identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Carafe demonstrates improved fragment ion intensity prediction and peptide detection relative to existing pretrained DDA models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Carafe demonstrates improved fragment ion intensity prediction and peptide detection relative to existing pretrained DDA models.
alignments.rationale: The result claim is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Carafe has been integrated into the widely used Skyline tool to enhance accessibility for the community.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Carafe has been integrated into the widely used Skyline tool to enhance accessibility for the community.
alignments.rationale: The integration claim is carried forward verbatim into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep learning-based methods were used to design Ras isoform-specific binders (RIBs) for all major Ras isoforms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep learning-based methods were used to design Ras isoform-specific binders (RIBs) targeting the Ras C terminus.
alignments.rationale: The publication refines the design target (Ras C terminus) while preserving the core contribution of DL-designed isoform-specific binders.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The RIBs bind to their target Ras isoforms both in vitro and in cells with remarkable specificity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The RIBs bind to their target Ras isoforms both in vitro and in cells with remarkable specificity.
alignments.rationale: Binding specificity in vitro and in cells is asserted identically in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The RIBs disrupt the membrane localization of Ras isoforms and inhibit Ras activity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The RIBs disrupt the membrane localization of Ras isoforms and inhibit Ras activity.
alignments.rationale: The functional effect on membrane localization and inhibition is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The designed RIBs should contribute to dissecting the distinct roles of Ras isoforms in biology and disease.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The RIBs enable dissection of the distinct roles of Ras isoforms during Ras<sup>G12C</sup> inhibitor resistance.
alignments.rationale: The publication narrows the stated utility to a specific context (G12C inhibitor resistance) rather than the broader biological/disease settings suggested in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Lupine is the first imputation method designed to learn jointly from many datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Lupine is the first imputation method designed to learn jointly from many datasets.
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is reproduced unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Lupine leads to more accurate predictions for imputing missing values in TMT proteomics data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Lupine leads to more accurate predictions for imputing missing values in quantitative proteomics data.
alignments.rationale: The publication generalizes the result from TMT-specific data to quantitative proteomics broadly, clarifying and broadening the scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Lupine outperforms the state of the art for TMT imputation and identifies more differentially expressed proteins than other methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Lupine outperforms the state of the art for proteomics imputation.
alignments.rationale: The core claim of outperforming the state of the art is retained but broadened from TMT-specific to proteomics generally; the publication omits the explicit phrasing about identifying more DE proteins in this specific claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Lupine corrects for TMT batch effects and learns a meaningful representation of proteins and patient samples.
alignments.rationale: The publication claims emphasize imputation performance and identification of differential signals but do not include an explicit claim about batch-effect correction or learned representations.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method combines continuous psychophysical estimation with machine learning clustering and transition probability analysis to study binocular rivalry.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The new method InFoRM-Rivalry avoids biases and assumptions present in conventional methods for studying binocular rivalry.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the contribution in terms of avoiding biases of conventional methods, which clarifies the benefit of the described combination of techniques.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This combination of techniques reveals individual variability and complexity of perceptual experience in normally sighted participants.
alignments.pub_claim_text: On average, 10 ± 8 perceptual categories arise during binocular rivalry, mapping predominantly onto exclusive and superimposed principal regions.
alignments.rationale: The publication gives a specific, quantitative characterization of perceptual categories, refining the preprint's more general claim about variability and complexity.
